cards.back: A concept in game theory where each player's strategy is optimal given the information available to them at any point in the game, and no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy.
A sub-game perfect equilibrium where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, and all players are making the best decision given the information available to them.
A smaller game or sub-game within a larger game, where the players' strategies may change in response to the outcome of the sub-game.
A set of possible states of the world that a player has information about, and may use this information to make their optimal strategy.
The idea that a player's strategy at any point in the game is optimal given the information available to them at that point, and any change to their strategy would make them worse off.
